2606.12260.title: Market Design for AI: Beyond the Copyright Binary
2606.12260.paper_summary: How can we design a market of human-generated content for use in training AI models that both enables technological progress and preserves individual incentives for high-quality content creation? Existing approaches take polar positions: a "free-for-all" model based on fair use and a "strong intellectual property rights" model. We show that both fail: Free-for-all does not compensate creators, and -- by modeling as a static Stackelberg game -- strong intellectual property rights also underpower creative incentives. We find this especially true for more innovative creators, a phenomenon we term the "originality penalty." Extending this insight to a dynamic model, we find another market failure undermining AI model performance, even for an initially good model: Such a model induces greater reliance by humans on AI-assisted creation, resulting in homogenized content feeding back into training, which degrades the model performance -- a "curse of precision." We further propose a market design with a data intermediary internalizing cross-creator externalities and subsidizing innovative contributions, thereby restoring efficiency.
2606.12260.paper_summary_zh: 如何設計一個人類生成內容的市場，以用於訓練AI模型，既能促進技術進步，又能保護個人創作高品質內容的動機？現有的方法採取極端立場：基於合理使用的「自由競爭」模式和「強知識產權」模式。我們顯示這兩者都失敗了：自由競爭無法補償創作者，而——通過將其建模為靜態的Stackelberg遊戲——強知識產權也削弱了創作的動機。我們發現這對於更具創新性的創作者尤其真實，這一現象我們稱之為「原創性懲罰」。將這一見解擴展到動態模型，我們發現另一種市場失靈削弱了AI模型的性能，即使對於最初表現良好的模型：這樣的模型促使人類對AI輔助創作的依賴加大，導致同質化內容反饋到訓練中，從而降低模型性能——這是一種「精確性的詛咒」。我們進一步提出一種市場設計，通過數據中介來內部化跨創作者的外部性並補貼創新貢獻，從而恢復效率。
2606.12260.author: Yan Dai et.al.
2606.12260.authors: Yan Dai, Maryam Farboodi, Negin Golrezaei, Sepehr Shahshahani
